schemas.headline: Understanding EM vs REM CSS Units
schemas.description: Learn the difference between EM and REM CSS units and how they cascade to create responsive typography.

mainEntity.name: How do I convert px to em?

acceptedAnswer.text: Divide the pixel value by the parent element's font size. If the parent font size is 16px (browser default), then 24px = 24/16 = 1.5em. Our calculator above does this conversion instantly — enter a pixel value and base font size to get the em equivalent.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between em and rem in CSS?

acceptedAnswer.text: em is relative to the parent element's font size — it compounds when nested. rem (root em) is always relative to the root html element's font size (usually 16px). Use rem for consistent sizing across your page, em when you want sizes to scale with parent context.
